Caption: Block diagram of the HESS (variable-speed turbine). Conceptually, the pump and fixed-speed turbine do not entail any meaningful difference.
Turbine mode: flow number (a) and efficiency (b) characteristic curves. The experimental points are marked with circles. All quantities are in per unit.
Pump mode: pressure number (a) and efficiency (b) characteristic curves. The experimental points are marked with circles. All quantities are in per unit.
Block diagram of the PSHP modules and their connections.
Recorded frequency (top) and AGC signal (bottom) for January 21, 2020 (a), and detail of the simulated hour (b).
Low-pass filters’ time constants for the variable-speed (top) and fixed-speed (bottom) plant. On the left-hand side: values of T l p f , h ; on the right-hand side: T l p f , b .
Active power comparison between two simulations: calibrated and incremented T l p f , b (Case 1 and 2 respectively).

Results for the variable-speed turbine: relative wicket gate’s travelled distance (a) and number of movements (b), BESS absolute (c) and relative (d) life consumption. L w g r e f = 0 . 7564 p . u . , N w g r e f = 137 .

Comparison between the electric power (top) and SOC (bottom) of FESS (a) and BESS (b) in different configurations. (a) comparison between the configurations (5, 0.5) MW and (0.5, 5) MW . (b) comparison between the configuration (5 5) MW and (0.5, 0.5) MW .
Results for the fixed-speed turbine. In the non-hybridized plant (reference case): L w g r e f = 0 . 7313 p . u . , N w g r e f = 122 .

Results for the variable-speed pump. (a) Mei-Wang fluctuation index of the torque at the shaft (reference case: M W F i r e f = 0 . 0106 ); (b) comparison of the torque between the (non-hybridized) reference case and the plant hybridized with 5 MW of BESS and FESS (top) and detail (bottom).

Comparison between the non-hybrid variable-speed turbine and the hybridized ( 5 MW of both BESS and FESS) fixed-speed turbine. (a) power plant’s (top) and PSHP (bottom) electric power output. (b) wicket gate position.
